title: Chees Clarifications
wikitext:
[]: Chee's Clarifications Post by chee » Wed Jun 09, 2010 1:47 pm Q: Why is Unit-02 Red? A: Ah, good question, but a very wrong one. As Deleuze once famously said, "There are not only wrong answers, but wrong questions." So we must rephrase the question, yes? It is of note that both Unit-02, Asuka's plugsuit, AND Asuka's hair are red. Why is this so? To uncover the hidden historiographical-semiotic relations embedded within the text by Anno, we must dig through a bit of background. The color red was invented in 1764 by Karl Marx in order to symbolize Communism and its original pacifist mascot Santa Claus. However, Communism fell when in 1962 color movies were invented by Thomas Edison, causing people to realize that blood is red. When they saw things in color for the first time, so they realized just how much blood was spilled in the name of Equal Holiday Cheer for All. Many of the great color painters like Picasso, Rodan and Mahler, were in fact insane visionaries who accessed a higher plane of existence through their psychosis. As we all know, Asuka is an uberfrau , the perfect Nietzschean archetype of the tragic, noble figure. What makes her so tragic and noble? Her embrace of Communism, and her silent acknowledgement of the risks and dangers its practice represents, in the hope of a better tomorrow. She is Badiou's ethical paradigm made flesh, the ideal militant. But who opposed this? The question leads to the FAR. As we all know, Neon Genesis Evangelion hit its stride when it decided to cast off unneeded "character development" and "dramatic tension" and focused soley on the mythology involving FAR, Adam, and Lillith. (This was considered a brilliant aesthetic and formal move by critics, who were puzzled by a series that would have its own seemingly murky and "secondarily important" mythology take back seat to the cliched practice of developing narrative and characters foremost.) Look closely at the below diagram: FAR Do you see it? Look again: F A R The center letter is a capital A . In classical aesthetics, the center of a frame composition is considered most important. Keeping this in mind, which historical figure is most famous for having a capital A in his name? That's right. Adolf Hitler . Seele ally themselves with the resolution of the FAR conflict through a realization of the FAR through Instrumentality... while based in Germany and using German names. The Nazis therefore were the first attempt to destroy the human race through instrumentality, and Seele are the revival of the Nazi movement. Asuka bravely opposed this tyranny through her embrace of Communism and defiance of this cancer within her home country. But wait! Was not Karl Marx also German? The answer is clear now: Unit-02 and Asuka are red out of reverence for her beloved ancestor Marx. She stands opposed to Seele and her goals- only to receive a vaguely phallic lance to the face. So, that answers your question. Tune in next time....

[]: Post by esselfortium » Wed Jun 09, 2010 1:49 pm Oh. I think I get it now. Thanks, Chee.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Jun 09, 2010 1:53 pm However, Communism fell when in 1962

[]: Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jun 09, 2010 1:56 pm FAR, Capital A, Adolf and AIDS! OMG, how's it I didn't think of that before! Everything is clear now, thank you sirrah.

[]: Post by tomrule123 » Wed Jun 09, 2010 2:41 pm Wait a minute... you mean to tell me that she... no, that can't be... REALLY? Could this all be coincidence? Well, this certainly changes my point-of-view on all of this.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Jun 09, 2010 2:49 pm After a second read, I find it more enlightening than Kotani's paper. Ciriclejerk post-structuralism FTW.

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jun 09, 2010 2:53 pm I was blind, but now I have seen the light. For opening my eyes to the hidden truths behind the color of unit-2 you have my eternal gratitude. I eagerly await your next earth shattering revelation.

[]: Post by Oz » Wed Jun 09, 2010 2:56 pm This is the greatest thread ever.

[]: Post by Allemann » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:00 pm If Chee would elaborate more on this, it would be legitimately publishable in a postmodernist journal, with Žižek's review in the next issue.

[]: Post by Merridian » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:03 pm By this logic, Unit-01's purple/green color scheme is representative of the ancient Roman Ayn Randius' "phallus incognito" concept, in which the individual masquerades as an enormous female equivalent of Moscow in order to combat the communist norms of the Yugo implication*, whereby all social constructs are merely products of an imaginative capitalist consciousness that manipulates the sex drive and gender dynamics of the feminist cyborgs present in the global economy. It all comes down to class gender warfare, she claims in her opus, You Can't Spell Manifesto Without "Man" , and although she makes a valid point, I think this only enables the pursuit of self-fulfilling prophecies present in the post-structuralist world of false historical narratives and the disillusionment of Foucault’s Pendulum. * the bipartisanship inherently present in the Yugo paradigm, aka "cheap cars" vs the Testla Engine that was eventually superseded by Henry Ford's light bulb predicament. Benjamin Franklin reasoned that the Testla Engine represented the prevailing wind of electronic maneuverability present in the overall narratological streambed of modern consciousness, but considering most just dismiss Franklin as being another sad result of the dustbins of history (I don't), some might consider this view bias. Especially since Ford's steam engine is now what powers the Sun in most places of the world... I'm certain Anno had all of this in mind, because his placing of Yui Ikari within this symbol of phallic reformation and fallacy only underlines the extreme feminist matrix present in modern Japan's--and by proxy, the modern world's--current social consciousness. Long story short, Anno predicted the collapse of the global economy and mass devastation of gender identity by 2012 by uniting the symbols of the ultimate Earth-feminine with the ultimate false phallo-centrific denial mechanism, and used this combination as a vehicle to facilitate the undermining of a patriarchal world view. I'm interested to see how you claim to resolve this prophetic sexual paradox of Asuka's Marxist beginnings with Shinji & Unit-01's ancient Roman masculo-capitalist idealism. EDIT Allemann wrote: If Chee would elaborate more on this, it would be legitimately publishable in a postmodernist journal, with Žižek's review in the next issue.

[]: Post by chee » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:05 pm Allemann wrote: If Chee would elaborate more on this, it would be legitimately publishable in a postmodernist journal, with Žižek's review in the next issue.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:07 pm Allemann wrote: Žižek's review

[]: Post by Allemann » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:16 pm chee wrote: Chomsky...

[]: Post by chee » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:25 pm Q: Why don't I understand the mythology in Eva? A: Because you're stupid. Q: Who's in Unit-00? A: Unit-00 is blue. Let's take a look at this. Unit-01 is purple... and so was Yui's shirt. Unit-02 is Red.... and Asuka's mother is assumedly a redhead. Unit-00 is Orange and Blue... ...so the only logical answer is Sonic and Tails. Since Kiel is a cyborg, we can assume he is in fact Dr. Robotnik after a particularly gruesome battle with Sonic.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Jun 09, 2010 3:40 pm DISCUSS

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jun 09, 2010 4:22 pm Image memes, politics and pedantry?; We're done here.
